executiveSummary.text: TJX is a high-quality off-price retail business with durable competitive advantages in buyer sourcing and inventory management, trading near the top of its historical valuation range and facing a moderately uncertain consumer backdrop, leaving it fairly valued at current levels and warranting a HOLD.

businessModel.text:
TJX is the world's largest off-price retailer, operating over 4,700 stores across four primary brands. Its competitive model is built on buying excess inventory from brand-name manufacturers at a significant discount and passing those savings on to consumers. Key business drivers:
    
      Marmaxx (T.J. Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods)~65% of revenue; US off-price apparel and home goods
      HomeSense / Homesense Canada~10% of revenue; broader home formats
      TJX Europe (TK Maxx, Homesense UK)~18% of revenue; international expansion
      TJX Canada (Winners, HomeSense, Marshalls)~7% of revenue
    
    TJX's buyer organisation is its core asset: hundreds of expert buyers who source brand-name goods at 20-60% below department store prices. The "treasure hunt" in-store experience drives customer visit frequency. TJX does not own manufacturing -- all inventory is purchased from third-party brands seeking to clear excess stock.

thesisEvaluation.text:
ScenarioScoreProbabilityCommentary
    
      Bull7830%Off-price share gains accelerate; comps grow 4-5% annually; European and Canadian expansion drives unit growth; TJX reaches $8+ EPS; re-rates to 36-38x, reaching $260+.
    
    
      Base7050%Moderate consumer spending slowdown offset by value migration; comps grow 2-3%; EPS reaches $5.50-6.00; shares drift higher with earnings, reaching $175-185 in 12 months.
    
    
      Bear5520%Recession triggers sharp consumer spending reduction; excess inventory available for TJX to buy shrinks; margin compression from tariff costs; EPS falls to $3.50-4.00; shares test $115-125.
    
  
  
    Conviction Score: 78x0.30 + 70x0.50 + 55x0.20 = 69.4
    Conviction falls at the top of the HOLD range. High quality is offset by elevated valuation and consumer cycle risk.

recommendation.text:
Rating: HOLD
    TJX is one of the highest-quality retail businesses in the world, with a sourcing moat, proven management team, and strong brand franchise. However, shares trade within 2.5% of their 52-week high at 33x earnings, which leaves little room for error. A meaningful decline to $140-145 would create a more attractive risk/reward for new orpositive momentum positions.
    
      FrameworkDetail
      Entry point$161.60 (current); prefer to wait for a pullback to $140-145 for improved entry
      Target price (12 months)$178-$188 based on 32-33x base case EPS of $5.50-$5.75
      Stop-loss$117 (below the 52-week low; would signal material consumer deterioration)
      Position sizingHigh-quality consumer discretionary holding; 1-1.5% portfolio weight appropriate
      Key trigger to upgradeComp store sales acceleration above 4%, confirmation that inventory supply remains robust, or meaningful tariff resolution reducing cost uncertainty

entryExit.text:
TJX Companies Inc (TJX) (TJX) — Entry / Exit Framework

HOLD zone: $148.67–$164.83 — current levels are fairly valued. No compelling reason to add or trim here.
Add / Initiate below $148.67 — material pullback that improves risk/reward.
Trim / Take Profits above $164.83 — approaching the 52-week high at $165.82. Risk/reward no longer favourable for new capital.
Stop-loss consideration: A sustained close below $117.44 (52-week low) would warrant reassessment.
